steps.title: Use the positive context
steps.content: The passage says the book's reception has been very good and notes that it won a literature award. The blank therefore needs a verb showing favorable support from reviewers, booksellers, and librarians.

steps.title: Reject negative verbs
steps.content: Choice A means criticized, the opposite of a good reception. Choice C means failed to notice, but the book received attention and an award. Choice D means failed to understand, which does not fit the praise implied by the context. The answer is B.
